title: Limits of combined functions: products and quotients
description: Find limits of combinations of two functions whose graphs are given.
topic_path: Determining limits using algebraic properties of limits: limit properties
Limits of combined functions: products and quotients

assessment_items.question_content:
Functions $f$ and $g$ are graphed.

![Function f is graphed. The x-axis goes from negative 4 to 4. The graph consists of a set of line segments. The set starts at (negative 4, 3), moves downward to (negative 3, negative 1), moves upward to (0, 1), moves downward through (2, 0) to (3, negative 1), moves upward and ends at (4, 3).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/783d3ced9350ee302afefa1a4e38bcb0ed149e0e) ![Function g is graphed. The x-axis goes from negative 4 to 4. The graph consists of a set of line segments. The set starts at (negative 4, 6), moves downward to (negative 1, 2), moves upward to (0, 4), moves downward through an open circle at (2, 2), and ends at (4, 0).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b1b7a116532d1e889b25096096faf9088f7c4d1c)

**Find $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to 2}\Big(f(x)g(x)\Big)$.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Putting it all together:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\lim_{x\to 2}\Big(f(x)g(x)\Big)
\\\\
&=\blueD{\lim_{x\to 2}f(x)}\cdot\maroonD{\lim_{x\to 2}g(x)}
\\\\
&=\blueD 0\cdot\maroonD 2
\\\\
&=0
\end{align}$

**Find $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to -1}\Big(h(x)f(x)\Big)$.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Putting it all together:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\lim_{x\to -1}\Big(h(x)f(x)\Big)
\\\\
&=\blueD{\lim_{x\to -1}h(x)}\cdot\maroonD{\lim_{x\to -1}f(x)}
\\\\
&=\blueD 2\cdot\maroonD 4
\\\\
&=8
\end{align}$

[[☃ radio 1]]

The first set starts at (negative 4, 0), moves upward to (negative 3, 4), moves horizontally to the right, and ends at an open circle at (negative 2, 4). The second set starts at a closed circle at (negative 2, 2), moves downward to (2, negative 1), moves upward and ends at (4, 1).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9047fbcfcb6efbed220b35c5fbd1a53d6d8061c4) ![Function h is graphed. The x-axis goes from negative 4 to 4. The graph consists of a set of line segments. The set starts at (negative 4, 2), moves horizontally to the right to (negative 2, 2), moves downward to (negative 1, 1), moves upward to (1, 3), moves downward to (3, negative 1), moves upward and ends at (4, 0).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1dc631ed58f6c97ba62b74b25f32e3df2e3f5a75)

**Find $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to -2}\Big(g(x)h(x)\Big)$.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's find the limit from the left:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\lim_{x\to -2^-}\Big(g(x)h(x)\Big)
\\\\
&=\blueD{\lim_{x\to -2^-}g(x)}\cdot\maroonD{\lim_{x\to -2^-}h(x)}
\\\\
&=\blueD 4\cdot\maroonD 2
\\\\
&=8
\end{align}$

Now let's find the limit from the right:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\lim_{x\to -2^+}\Big(g(x)h(x)\Big)
\\\\
&=\purpleD{\lim_{x\to -2^+}g(x)}\cdot\maroonD{\lim_{x\to -2^+}h(x)}
\\\\
&=\purpleD 2\cdot\maroonD 2
\\\\
&=4
\end{align}$

The one-sided limits are different and therefore the limit doesn't exist.

The x-axis goes from negative 4 to 4. The graph consists of 2 sets of line segments. The first set starts at (negative 4, negative 1), moves upward and ends at an open circle at (0, 3). The second set starts at a closed circle at (0, 0), moves downward to (2, negative 1), moves upward and ends at (4, 1).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d78f77c1f0c09e3ac8c0739528c18237b3972567)

**Find $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to 0}\Big(f(x)h(x)\Big)$.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's find the limit from the left:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\lim_{x\to 0^-}\Big(f(x)h(x)\Big)
\\\\
&=\blueD{\lim_{x\to 0^-}f(x)}\cdot\maroonD{\lim_{x\to 0^-}h(x)}
\\\\
&=\blueD 1\cdot\maroonD 3
\\\\
&=3
\end{align}$

Now let's find the limit from the right:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\lim_{x\to 0^+}\Big(f(x)h(x)\Big)
\\\\
&=\blueD{\lim_{x\to 0^+}f(x)}\cdot\goldD{\lim_{x\to 0^+}h(x)}
\\\\
&=\blueD 1\cdot\goldD 0
\\\\
&=0
\end{align}$

The one-sided limits are different and therefore the limit doesn't exist.

**Find $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to -3}\Big(g(x)f(x)\Big)$.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's find the limit from the left:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\lim_{x\to -3^-}\Big(g(x)f(x)\Big)
\\\\
&=\blueD{\lim_{x\to -3^-}g(x)}\cdot\maroonD{\lim_{x\to -3^-}f(x)}
\\\\
&=\blueD 1\cdot\maroonD 0
\\\\
&=0
\end{align}$

Now let's find the limit from the right:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\lim_{x\to -3^+}\Big(g(x)f(x)\Big)
\\\\
&=\purpleD{\lim_{x\to -3^+}g(x)}\cdot\maroonD{\lim_{x\to -3^+}f(x)}
\\\\
&=\purpleC {-1}\cdot\maroonD 0
\\\\
&=0
\end{align}$

The one-sided limits are both equal to $0$ and therefore $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to -3}\Big(g(x)f(x)\Big)=0$.

The line segment starts at an open circle at (3, 1), moves upward and ends at (4, 3).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/dea9a5a7ce35feb61379c0aacacb8f0cc8b244c1)

**Find $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to 3}\Big(h(x)g(x)\Big)$.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's find the limit from the left:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\lim_{x\to 3^-}\Big(h(x)g(x)\Big)
\\\\
&=\blueD{\lim_{x\to 3^-}h(x)}\cdot\maroonD{\lim_{x\to 3^-}g(x)}
\\\\
&=\blueD 1\cdot\maroonD 5
\\\\
&=5
\end{align}$

Now let's find the limit from the right:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\lim_{x\to 3^+}\Big(h(x)g(x)\Big)
\\\\
&=\purpleD{\lim_{x\to 3^+}h(x)}\cdot\goldD{\lim_{x\to 3^+}g(x)}
\\\\
&=\purpleD 5\cdot\goldD 1
\\\\
&=5
\end{align}$

The one-sided limits are both equal to $5$ and therefore $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to 3}\Big(h(x)g(x)\Big)=5$.
